data.title: Saab reports strong sales growth and market position improvement in Q2 2025, investing for future success.
data.description: Saab, a company based in Stockholm, announced their financial results for the first half of 2025. The report highlighted their strong market presence and the increasing demand for their products and solutions. With a significant sales growth in the specified period, Saab is focused on expanding its capacity and resources to cater to the expanding market interest. The company continues to invest in its operations to ensure sustainability and long-term success.

data.title: June CPI rose 0.3%, annual CPI climbed 2.9%, while core inflation increased by 2.7%. Economist sees this as good news.
data.description: Prominent economists are analyzing the recent Consumer Price Index report from June, which indicated that inflation was consistent with forecasts. The U.S. witnessed a 0.3% increase in consumer prices for June, following a 0.1% rise in May. On a yearly basis, the Consumer Price Index surged by 2.9%, with core inflation (excluding food and energy) increasing by 2.7% from the previous year. Notably, University of Michigan economist Justin Wolfers viewed the results optimistically, commenting that "The absence of bad news is good news," and highlighting that the figures aligned closely with expectations. This development sparks speculation about Federal Reserve policy shifts and the mounting pressures from tariffs. Recent data shows a 0.3% uptick in headline CPI for May, with a 2.7% increase over the past year. Core CPI also expanded by 0.2%, reaching a 2.9% growth rate. These numbers largely align with projections, indicating a stable economic trend. For more details, refer to the comprehensive coverage on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Munters Group AB divests FoodTech Equipment offering for solid growth and robust margin; April-June 2025 order intake rises.
data.description: In KISTA, Sweden on July 18, 2025, the divestment of the FoodTech Equipment offering has been successfully finalized. The details and statistics provided in this statement are related to ongoing operations unless specified otherwise. Further information can be found on pages 16-17. During the period of April to June 2025, there was a notable rise in order intake.

data.title: Boliden's Q2 2025 report shows stable production despite challenges. Revenues: SEK 22,285m. CEO comments on strong cash flow.
data.description: In a recent announcement on July 18, 2025, Mikael Staffas, the President and CEO of the company, highlighted the robust underlying cash flow and stable production levels. This is noteworthy considering the difficult market circumstances and significant planned maintenance activities undertaken in the quarter. The financial report for the period revealed revenues of SEK 22,285 million, slightly lower compared to the previous period’s SEK 22,748 million.

data.title: Truecaller AB reports a 9% increase in net sales to SEK 496.4 million, highlighting strong revenue and user growth.
data.description: In their interim report for January-June 2025, Truecaller, a prominent global platform for contact verification and spam call blocking, announced a 9% rise in net sales to SEK 496.4 million. This increase from SEK 455.9 million was achieved despite fluctuations in currency values, indicating positive growth for the company.

data.title: SKF Q2 2025: Net sales MSEK 23,166, organic growth -0.2%, adjusted operating profit MSEK 3,090. Automotive market affected.
data.description: In Gothenburg, Sweden, as of July 18, 2025, the net sales for Q2 2025 amounted to MSEK 23,166, showing a decrease from the previous period's MSEK 25,606. The organic growth rate saw a slight decline at -0.2% compared to the previous -6.6%. This shift was primarily due to decreased market demand in the Automotive sector, while the Industrial segment experienced growth in organic sales. The adjusted operating profit for the quarter was MSEK 3,090, down from MSEK 3,324 in the same period.

data.title: Whale from Satoshi era moves 40,000 BTC to Galaxy Digital. Speculations suggest securing profits after 14 years.
data.description: Kadan Stadelmann, the Chief Technology Officer of Komodo Platform, suggests that a large investor may be taking action to secure its significant profits after holding onto them for a period of 14 years.

data.title: Strong first half for 2025 with net profit of DKK 11.2 billion due to good customer activity and credit quality.
data.description: Positive customer engagement and robust creditworthiness contributed to successful performance in the initial six months of 2025, with a net profit of DKK 11.2 billion achieved.

data.title: GENIUS Act to overhaul stablecoin regulation, awaits Trump's approval, set to reform operations in US and worldwide.
data.description: The GENIUS Act, focused on regulating stablecoins, is on its way to be signed by Donald Trump. This is likely to significantly impact the operations of stablecoins both within the US and globally.
